name: Goat Cheese and Spinach Turnovers
recipeIngredient: 2 Bunches fresh spinach, stemmed and chopped
2 Ounces Soft fresh GOAT CHEESE (Chevre/Moncail Log)
3 Tablespoons Grated Parmesan or Romano (Goat if Available)
1/2 Teaspoon minced FRESH Rosemary or 1/4 Teaspoon dried crumbled Rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add onion and garlic and sauté until translucent (about 5 minutes).
3. Increase the heat to high and add spinach, cooking until wilted (about 5 minutes).
4. Drain the spinach mixture, pressing to release as much moisture as possible. Place in a large bowl and cool completely.
5. Add GOAT CHEESE, pine nuts, Parmesan or Romano, rosemary, and lime zest. Season with sea salt and fresh ground pepper.
6. Place 1 phyllo sheet on the work surface. Cut lengthwise into 3 strips and brush with melted butter.
7. Place 1 tablespoon of filling at one end of the dough strip. Start at one corner, fold the pastry over the filling, forming a triangle. Repeat folding up the length of the pastry as though folding a flag. Brush with butter.
8. Repeat steps 6-7 with remaining phyllo sheets, butter, and filling.
9. Transfer turnovers to a non-stick baking sheet. Cover and chill for 2 hours.
10. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and bake turnovers until golden brown, about 10-12 minutes.
11. Serve hot.
NOTE: Adding 1 pound of cooked ground lamb or goat meat makes this a meal in itself.
